agency: New Mexico Department of Game and Fish (NMDGF)

files_downloaded.description: 2025 Drawing Odds Summary Report - summary draw odds for all big game species including deer and elk (2025 draw cycle)
2025 Drawing Odds Complete Report - detailed draw odds by hunt code for all species including deer and elk (2025 draw cycle)
2024 Drawing Odds Summary Report - summary draw odds for all big game species including deer and elk (2024 draw cycle)
2024 Drawing Odds Complete Report - detailed draw odds by hunt code for all species including deer and elk (2024 draw cycle)
2024-2025 Elk Harvest Report (corrected) - harvest data by GMU for NM elk hunts
2024-2025 Deer Harvest Report - harvest data by GMU for NM deer hunts
2023-2024 Deer Harvest Report - harvest data by GMU for NM deer hunts (prior year)
NMDGF Draw Info, Odds & Success Tips page - explains NM draw system, quota allocation (84% resident, 6% nonresident, 10% outfitter)
2024 draw results announcement page - 287,449 applications submitted, 61,341 draw licenses issued

needs_manual.description: Online Draw Results Lookup - individual draw result checker
needs_manual.reason: Interactive web app requiring user login/search; not a downloadable data file

needs_manual.description: WPDM download pages require browser interaction; direct file URLs include time-based refresh tokens that may expire
needs_manual.reason: If re-downloading is needed, visit the download page in a browser and click the download button

notes: NM uses a true random draw system with NO preference or bonus points - all applicants have equal odds within their pool.
Draw quota allocation: 84% resident, 6% nonresident (unguided), 10% outfitter-guided nonresident.
The Excel draw odds files (Summary and Complete reports) contain draw odds for ALL big game species: deer, elk, antelope, oryx, ibex, bighorn sheep, Barbary sheep, and javelina.
The Complete reports have per-hunt-code detail; the Summary reports have aggregated odds.
2024 draw: 287,449 total applications submitted, 61,341 draw licenses issued across all species.
The NMDGF website uses WordPress Download Manager (WPDM) which obscures direct file URLs behind download pages. The download URLs contain time-sensitive 'refresh' tokens.
Harvest reports are published as PDFs with data broken down by Game Management Unit (GMU).
A 2023-2024 elk harvest report may exist but was not found; the 2024-2025 elk report (corrected) was the most recent available.
All files were verified as genuine data files (Excel 2007+ or PDF) using file type detection.
